232	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Quot/Grassmannian predicates
233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
235	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
236	predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local
237	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/
238	
239	namespace SheafOfModules
240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules
259	
260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
264	§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
265	annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
280	
281	namespace Scheme.Modules
282	
283	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
284	
285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300	
301	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
303	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
308	
309	/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme
314	
315	/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
316	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
317	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
318	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
319	`annihilator` is. -/
320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
321	  (annihilator F).subschemeι
322	
323	/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
324	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
325	`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
326	base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
327	pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
329	  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)
330	
331	end Scheme.Modules
332	
333	end AlgebraicGeometry
334	
335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]
381	    | smul c a _ ha => rw [smul_comm, ha, smul_zero]
382	  apply le_antisymm
383	  · -- `Ann Rₚ Mₚ ⊆ (Ann R M).map`: clear one common denominator over the generators
384	    intro y hy
385	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at hy
386	    obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalization.mk'_surjective S y
387	    dsimp only at hy ⊢
388	    have hgen : ∀ m ∈ t, ∃ u : S, (u : R) • a • m = 0 := by
389	      intro m hm
390	      have hz := hy (IsLocalizedModule.mk' f m (1 : S))
391	      rw [IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_eq_zero,
392	        IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S f] at hz
393	      obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hz
394	      exact ⟨u, hu⟩
395	    choose u hu using hgen
396	    obtain ⟨U, hU⟩ : ∃ U : S, ∀ m ∈ t, (U : R) • a • m = 0 := by
397	      refine ⟨∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2, ?_⟩
398	      intro m hm
399	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ :=
400	        Finset.dvd_prod_of_mem (fun x : t => u x.1 x.2) (Finset.mem_attach t ⟨m, hm⟩)
401	      have hcoe : ((∏ x ∈ t.attach, u x.1 x.2 : S) : R) = (u m hm : R) * (c : R) := by
402	        rw [hc]; push_cast; ring
403	      rw [hcoe, mul_smul, smul_comm, hu m hm, smul_zero]
404	    have hUa : (U : R) * a ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
405	      apply key; intro m hm; rw [mul_smul]; exact hU m hm
406	    have heq : IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ a s
407	        = (algebraMap R Rₚ ((U : R) * a)) * IsLocalization.mk' Rₚ 1 (U * s) := by
408	      rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_eq_mul_mk'_one, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq]
409	      push_cast; ring
410	    rw [heq]
411	    exact Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hUa)
412	  · -- `(Ann R M).map ⊆ Ann Rₚ Mₚ`: the image of an annihilator annihilates
413	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
414	    intro a ha
415	    rw [Ideal.mem_comap, Module.mem_annihilator]
416	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator] at ha
417	    intro x
418	    obtain ⟨⟨m, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f x
419	    dsimp only [Function.uncurry]
420	    rw [← IsLocalization.mk'_one (M := S) Rₚ a, IsLocalizedModule.mk'_smul_mk' Rₚ f, ha m,
421	      IsLocalizedModule.mk'_zero]
422	
423	end Module
424	
425	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections localize on a basic open
426	
427	This section builds, bottom-up, toward the keystone
428	`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`: for a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `M`
429	on a scheme `X`, an affine open `U`, and `f ∈ Γ(X,U)`, the restriction
430	`M(U) → M(D(f))` exhibits `M(D(f))` as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `Γ(X,U)`.
431	
432	The substance is the affine (Spec-local) computation: over `Spec R`, a quasi-coherent
433	sheaf is `Ñ = tilde N` for `N = Γ(M)`, and the basic-open restriction of `Ñ` is the
434	module localization map. Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde` namespace already carries
435	the localization fact for `tilde N` *as the map out of `N`* (the instance
436	`IsLocalizedModule (.powers f) (tilde.toOpen N (basicOpen f)).hom`). The first building
437	block below repackages this as a statement about the *presheaf restriction map* of `Ñ`
438	itself (from global sections to `D(f)`), which is the form the downstream scheme-level
439	argument consumes after the affine identification `M|_U ≅ Ñ`.
440	
441	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does **not** prove that an arbitrary quasi-coherent sheaf
442	on `Spec R` lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the comment in
443	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Tilde.lean` says this "will later be shown"); the
444	equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` is a genuine gap. Consequently the keystone for an
445	*arbitrary* quasi-coherent `M` is gated on that bridge (`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso
446	M.fromTildeΓ`); the building blocks here are stated for `tilde N` directly, and for a
447	general `M : (Spec R).Modules` under the explicit hypothesis `[IsIso M.fromTildeΓ]`
448	(equivalently, `M` in the essential image of `tilde`). -/
449	
450	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
451	
452	open CategoryTheory Limits
453	
454	/-- **Basic-open restriction of a `tilde` sheaf is a module localization.**
455	
456	For `N : ModuleCat R` and `f : R`, the presheaf restriction map of the associated sheaf
457	`Ñ = tilde N` from global sections `Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(Ñ, D(f))` exhibits the
458	latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
459	
460	This is the affine, Spec-local heart of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. Mathlib
461	carries the localization fact for the map `tilde.toOpen N (D f) : N → Γ(Ñ, D(f))` out of
462	`N`; since `tilde.toOpen N ⊤ : N → Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is an isomorphism and
463	`tilde.toOpen N (D f) = tilde.toOpen N ⊤ ≫ restriction` (`tilde.toOpen_res`), precomposing
464	the localization map with the inverse isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`)
465	transfers the property to the restriction map. Project-local: Mathlib states the fact only
466	for the map out of `N`, not for the presheaf restriction of `Ñ`. -/
467	theorem isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : ModuleCat.{u} R) (f : R) :
468	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
469	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
470	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
471	  set res := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde N)).presheaf.map
472	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op with hresdef
473	  have hres := tilde.toOpen_res N ⊤ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
474	    (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤))
475	  -- `e : N ≃ₗ Γ(Ñ, ⊤)` is the global-sections isomorphism of the tilde sheaf.
476	  set e : N ≃ₗ[R] _ := (tilde.isoTop N).toLinearEquiv with hedef
477	  have key : (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom = res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap := by
478	    rw [hedef, ← hres]; rfl
479	  have hinst0 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
480	      (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom := inferInstance
481	  rw [key] at hinst0
482	  set g := res.hom ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap with hg
483	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) g := hinst0
484	  have h2 := IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := g) e.symm
485	  have he : g ∘ₗ e.symm.toLinearMap = res.hom := by
486	    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
487	    change res.hom (e (e.symm x)) = res.hom x
488	    rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
489	  rw [he] at h2
490	  exact h2
491	
492	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a sheaf in the essential image of `tilde`.**
493	
494	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` whose tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an
495	isomorphism (equivalently, `M` lies in the essential image of the `tilde` functor — the
496	honest Spec-affine stand-in for quasi-coherence, see the section header), the presheaf
497	restriction map of `M` from global sections `Γ(M, ⊤)` to the basic open `Γ(M, D(f))`
498	exhibits the latter as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
499	
500	This transports `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict` across the isomorphism
501	`M.fromTildeΓ : tilde N ⟶ M` (where `N = Γ(M, ⊤)`): the induced presheaf isomorphism is
502	natural in the open, so on each of `⊤` and `D(f)` it provides an `R`-linear isomorphism
503	intertwining the two restriction maps. Post- and pre-composing the localization map for
504	`tilde N` with these isomorphisms (`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv`,
505	`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`) yields the property for `M`.
506	
507	Project-local: it is the affine engine of `lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`. The
508	general quasi-coherent case additionally requires the (currently Mathlib-absent) bridge
509	`IsQuasicoherent M → IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. -/
510	theorem isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
511	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) [IsIso M.fromTildeΓ] (f : R) :
512	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
513	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
514	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
515	  -- the presheaf-level isomorphism induced by the (iso) counit `M.fromTildeΓ`
516	  let ψ := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget (ModuleCat R) (Spec R)).map (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ)
517	  haveI : IsIso ψ := inferInstance
518	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens))) := inferInstance
519	  haveI : IsIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))) := inferInstance
520	  -- the component isomorphisms as `R`-linear equivalences
521	  let eTop : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (⊤ : (Spec R).Opens)))).toLinearEquiv
522	  let eDf : _ ≃ₗ[R] _ := (asIso (ψ.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))).toLinearEquiv
523	  -- the restriction map of `tilde N` (localizes by `isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict`)
524	  let rt := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj
525	        (tilde ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)))).presheaf.map
526	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom
527	  -- naturality square of `ψ` for `D(f) ⟶ ⊤`
528	  have hnat := ψ.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op
529	  have hnathom := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hnat
530	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp] at hnathom
531	  haveI hrt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) rt :=
532	    isLocalizedModule_tilde_restrict ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤)) f
533	  haveI step1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) :=
534	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv (S := Submonoid.powers f) (f := rt) (e := eDf)
535	  haveI step2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
536	      ((eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) ∘ₗ eTop.symm.toLinearMap) :=
537	    IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (S := Submonoid.powers f)
538	      (f := eDf.toLinearMap ∘ₗ rt) (e := eTop.symm)
539	  -- identify the target restriction map with `(eDf ∘ rt) ∘ eTop⁻¹`
540	  convert step2 using 1
541	  apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
542	  have hc := LinearMap.congr_fun hnathom (eTop.symm x)
543	  simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply] at hc ⊢
544	  refine (?_ : _ = _).trans hc.symm
545	  congr 1
546	  exact (eTop.apply_symm_apply x).symm
547	
548	/-- A morphism of sheaves of `R`-modules on `Spec R` that is an isomorphism on every basic open
549	`D(f)` is an isomorphism. This is the "isomorphism on a basis ⟹ isomorphism" reduction specialised
550	to the basic-open basis of `Spec R` (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`): injectivity on stalks is
551	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`, surjectivity on stalks is the basic-open germ lift, and
552	`isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` concludes. Project-local glue used to assemble `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`
553	from per-basic-open section data. -/
554	private theorem isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
555	    {F G : TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat.{u} R) (Spec R)} (α : F ⟶ G)
556	    (h : ∀ f : R, IsIso (α.1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) : IsIso α := by
557	  have hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _)) :=
558	    PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
559	  have hinj : ∀ U ∈ Set.range (@PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen R _),
560	      Function.Injective (α.1.app (.op U)) := by
561	    rintro U ⟨f, rfl⟩
562	    exact ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).1
563	  have hstalk : ∀ x, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor (ModuleCat.{u} R) x).map α.1) := by
564	    intro x
565	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
566	    refine ⟨TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB hinj x, ?_⟩
567	    intro t
568	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hUB, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB G.presheaf x t
569	    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := hUB
570	    obtain ⟨s', rfl⟩ := ((ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective _).mp (h f)).2 s
571	    exact ⟨F.presheaf.germ _ x hxU s', by rw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]⟩
572	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso α
573	
574	/-- A linear map intertwining two localizations of the same module at the same submonoid is
575	bijective: if `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : M →ₗ M''` both exhibit a localization at `S` and
576	`h : M' →ₗ M''` satisfies `h ∘ₗ f = g`, then `h` is bijective (it is the canonical localization
577	isomorphism `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`). Stated with the two `IsLocalizedModule` facts as
578	explicit hypotheses to avoid typeclass-diamond ambiguity at the call site. -/
579	private theorem bijective_comp_of_localizations {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
580	    {M M' M'' : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
581	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module R M''] {f : M →ₗ[R] M'} {g : M →ₗ[R] M''} {h : M' →ₗ[R] M''}
582	    (hf : IsLocalizedModule S f) (hg : IsLocalizedModule S g) (hh : h ∘ₗ f = g) :
583	    Function.Bijective h := by
584	  haveI := hf; haveI := hg
585	  have heq : h = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).toLinearMap := by
586	    apply IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext S (f := f) (f' := g)
587	    apply LinearMap.ext
588	    intro x
589	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, ← LinearMap.comp_apply, hh,
590	      LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearMap, IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
591	  rw [heq]
592	  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S f g).bijective
593	
594	/-- **`IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` from per-basic-open section localization** (the cheap stalk/section
595	assembly of `lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the blueprint "G1-assemble" step). If for every
596	`f : R` the section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` of a sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R`
597	exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R` — exactly the conclusion of G1-core
598	(`lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`,
599	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`) — then the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is
600	an isomorphism (equivalently `M` lies in the essential image of `tilde`).
601	
602	On each basic open `D(f)` the component of `modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ` is a map between
603	two localizations of `N = Γ(M, ⊤)` at `powers f`: the source `Γ(tilde N, D(f))` localizes via the
604	instance `tilde.toOpen N (D f)` and the target `Γ(M, D(f))` localizes by hypothesis, and the two
605	localization maps are intertwined by `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app`. Hence the component is
606	the canonical localization isomorphism (`IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`);
607	`isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen` upgrades this to an isomorphism of sheaves, and
608	`modulesSpecToSheaf` being fully faithful reflects it to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`.
609	
610	This turns the remaining keystone obligation into exactly G1-core: combined with the converse engine
611	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`, the per-basic-open localization hypothesis is
612	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ`. Project-local: Mathlib has no `IsQuasicoherent → IsIso
613	fromTildeΓ` bridge. -/
614	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
615	    (M : (Spec R).Modules)
616	    (H : ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
617	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
618	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom) :
619	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := by
620	  haveI hmain : IsIso (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ) := by
621	    apply isIso_sheaf_of_isIso_app_basicOpen
622	    intro f
623	    set N := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj (.op ⊤) with hN
624	    -- target localizes by hypothesis; source localizes by the `tilde.toOpen` instance
625	    haveI htgt : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
626	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
627	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := H f
628	    set comp := (modulesSpecToSheaf.map M.fromTildeΓ).1.app (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f))
629	      with hcomp
630	    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
631	    have hcompose := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)
632	    -- the component intertwines the two localization maps of `N` at `powers f`
633	    have h1 : comp.hom ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom
634	        = ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
635	          (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
636	      have e := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom hcompose
637	      rwa [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at e
638	    change Function.Bijective (⇑comp.hom)
639	    exact bijective_comp_of_localizations (Submonoid.powers f)
640	      (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule _ (tilde.toOpen N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)).hom)
641	      (H f) h1
642	  exact SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful.isIso_of_isIso_map M.fromTildeΓ
643	
644	/-- **Characterization of `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` by section localization.** For a sheaf of modules
645	`M` on `Spec R`, the tilde-Gamma counit `M.fromTildeΓ` is an isomorphism iff for every `f : R` the
646	section restriction `Γ(M, ⊤) → Γ(M, D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
647	
648	The forward direction is the affine engine `isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`; the
649	reverse is `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict`. Combined with G1-core
650	(`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization`, not yet
651	formalized) — which supplies the right-hand side for any quasi-coherent `M` — this yields gap1
652	(`lem:qcoh_affine_isIso_fromTildeΓ`). Project-local. -/
653	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
654	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
655	    IsIso M.fromTildeΓ ↔ ∀ f : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
656	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
657	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom :=
658	  ⟨fun h f => by haveI := h; exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f,
659	    isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isLocalizedModule_restrict M⟩

661	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — G1-core (Route F) building blocks
662	
663	The keystone G1-core `lem:qcoh_affine_section_localization` asks: for a *quasi-coherent*
664	`M : (Spec R).Modules` and `f : R`, the section restriction `Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` is
665	`IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`. Via `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizedModule_restrict` this is
666	*equivalent* to `IsIso M.fromTildeΓ` — i.e. to the statement that a quasi-coherent sheaf on an
667	affine scheme lies in the essential image of `tilde` (the `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` equivalence). That
668	equivalence is a genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit (Tilde.lean only proves the *globally
669	presented* case, `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
670	
671	The composition lemma below discharges the **globally-presented** sub-case end to end (it is the
672	Route-F endpoint once a global presentation/tilde identification is in hand). The residual gap is
673	exactly the production of a global presentation/tilde from local (quasi-coherent) data on `Spec R`;
674	see the handoff in `task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
675	
676	/-- **Basic-open restriction localizes, for a globally presented module.** If `M : (Spec R).Modules`
677	admits a global `SheafOfModules.Presentation`, then for every `f : R` the section restriction
678	`Γ(M,⊤) → Γ(M,D(f))` exhibits the target as `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` over `R`.
679	
680	This is the composition of Mathlib's `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation` (a global presentation
681	forces `M.fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism, i.e. `M` is a `tilde`) with the affine engine
682	`isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ`. It is the Route-F endpoint for the
683	globally-presented case; the general quasi-coherent case additionally requires producing a global
684	presentation/tilde identification from the (Mathlib-absent) affine `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` bridge.
685	Project-local. -/
686	theorem isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_presentation {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
687	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (_P : M.Presentation) (f : R) :
688	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
689	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.map
690	        (homOfLE (le_top : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f ≤ ⊤)).op).hom := by
691	  haveI : IsIso M.fromTildeΓ := isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation M _P
692	  exact isLocalizedModule_restrict_of_isIso_fromTildeΓ M f
693	
694	/-- **`map_units` field of G1-core (Route F), for any sheaf of modules.** For `M : (Spec R).Modules`
695	and `f : R`, every element of `Submonoid.powers f` acts invertibly on the sections `Γ(M, D(f))` over
696	`R`. This is exactly the first field `IsLocalizedModule.map_units` of the target
697	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_of_isQuasicoherent`, in the shape the 3-field constructor consumes.
698	
699	It holds for an *arbitrary* `M` (no quasi-coherence needed): on `D(f)` the element `f` already maps
700	to a unit of the structure ring `Γ(O_{Spec R}, D(f))` (the away-localization
701	`IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`), and the `R`-action on `Γ(M, D(f))` factors through it via
702	the scalar tower `R → Γ(O, D(f)) → Γ(M, D(f))`. Packaged from Mathlib's
703	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen`. Project-local only as the *named* field
704	of the Route-F decomposition; the substance of G1-core is `surj`/`exists_of_eq` (see handoff). -/
705	theorem map_units_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : (Spec R).Modules) (f : R) :
706	    ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f, IsUnit (algebraMap R (Module.End R
707	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj M).presheaf.obj
708	        (.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f)))) (x : R)) := by
709	  rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
710	  simpa using (tilde.isUnit_algebraMap_end_basicOpen M f).pow n
711	
712	/-- **Finite basic-open cover refining a quasi-coherent presentation cover.** Given a
713	sheaf of modules `M` on `Spec R` together with quasi-coherent data `q` (a — possibly
714	infinite — open cover `q.X : q.I → (Spec R).Opens` of `⊤` with a presentation of
715	`M.over (q.X i)` on each member), there is a *finite* family of elements `t : Finset R`
716	whose basic opens cover `Spec R` (`Ideal.span t = ⊤`), with each basic open `D(r)`
717	(`r ∈ t`) contained in some member `q.X i` of the presentation cover.
718	
719	This is the topological "finite-cover front" of `lem:exists_isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_cover`:
720	quasi-compactness of `Spec R` plus the basic-open basis (`PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`)
721	refine `q.X` to a finite basic-open subcover; the cover condition `q.coversTop` is read off the
722	`Opens.grothendieckTopology` sieve via `Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff`, and finiteness is extracted
723	through `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. To obtain `q` from `[M.IsQuasicoherent]`, take
724	`‹M.IsQuasicoherent›.nonempty_quasicoherentData.some`.
725	
726	The remaining (heavy) step toward gap1 — transporting each presentation `q.presentation i`
727	of `M.over (q.X i)` across `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` to `IsIso ((M|_{D(r)}).fromTildeΓ)` — is the
728	site-slice ↔ scheme-pullback transport, which has no Mathlib support at the pinned commit.
729	Project-local: Mathlib has no affine quasi-coherent → finite presentation cover lemma. -/
730	theorem exists_finite_basicOpen_cover_le_quasicoherentData {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
731	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
732	    ∃ t : Finset R, Ideal.span (t : Set R) = ⊤ ∧
733	      ∀ r ∈ t, ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i := by
734	  classical
735	  set G : Set R := {r | ∃ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i} with hG
736	  have hspanG : Ideal.span G = ⊤ := by
737	    rw [← PrimeSpectrum.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top_iff']
738	    rw [eq_top_iff]
739	    intro x _
740	    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
741	    obtain ⟨U, f, hf, hxU⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ x (by trivial)
742	    rw [Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff] at hf
743	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hUi⟩⟩ := hf
744	    have hxXi : x ∈ q.X i := (leOfHom hUi) hxU
745	    obtain ⟨V, ⟨r, rfl⟩, hxV, hVle⟩ :=
746	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens) hxXi
747	    exact ⟨r, ⟨i, hVle⟩, hxV⟩
748	  obtain ⟨t, htsub, htspan⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite G).mp hspanG
749	  exact ⟨t, htspan, fun r hr => htsub hr⟩

751	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence
752	
753	The gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso` (`overRestrictIso`) rests on an
754	equivalence of *sheaf* categories
755	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`
756	induced by the equivalence of underlying sites
757	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`. Mathlib carries `Opens.overEquivalence` but leaves
758	the *continuity* of its two functors and the induced sheaf-category equivalence as an explicit TODO
759	(see `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`: "show that both functors of the equivalence
760	`overEquivalence U` are continuous and induce an equivalence between
761	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A` and `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology U) A`").
762	
763	This section fills that TODO. The two cover-lifting (`IsCocontinuous`) facts are the substance:
764	a sieve covers in the Grothendieck-topology-over-`U` exactly when its image under the slice
765	forgetful functor covers in the ambient space, and that condition matches the pointwise covering
766	condition on the open subspace `↥U` because `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is an injective open embedding.
767	From the two cocontinuities, `Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous` produces the
768	dense-subsite hypothesis and `Equivalence.sheafCongr` produces the sheaf equivalence.
769	
770	It is the foundational (purely topological / topos-theoretic) layer of the slice-to-geometric
771	transport; the remaining steps toward `overRestrictIso` (identifying the sliced structure sheaf
772	`O_X.over U` with the open subscheme's structure sheaf `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf` under this
773	equivalence, then lifting to sheaves of modules via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` and relating
774	to `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor U.ι`) are the geometric layer above it. -/
775	
776	section OverSiteSheafEquivalence
777	
778	open TopologicalSpace Topology
779	
780	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X)
781	
782	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
783	`U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck topology to the Grothendieck topology of the open
784	subspace `↥U`. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
785	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
786	instance overEquivalence_functor_isCocontinuous :
787	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsCocontinuous
788	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) where
789	  cover_lift := by
790	    intro Y S hS
791	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
792	    intro x hx
793	    have hxU : x ∈ U := leOfHom Y.hom hx
794	    have hmem : (⟨x, hxU⟩ : ↥U) ∈ (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj Y := hx
795	    obtain ⟨V, h, hSh, hxV⟩ := hS ⟨x, hxU⟩ hmem
796	    have hVle : (V : Set ↥U) ⊆ Subtype.val ⁻¹' (Y.left : Set X) := leOfHom h
797	    set W : Opens X := ⟨Subtype.val '' (V : Set ↥U),
798	      (U.isOpenEmbedding'.isOpen_iff_image_isOpen).1 V.isOpen⟩ with hWdef
799	    have hWle : W ≤ Y.left := by
800	      intro y hy; obtain ⟨z, hzV, rfl⟩ := hy; exact hVle hzV
801	    refine ⟨W, homOfLE hWle, ?_, ⟨⟨x, hxU⟩, hxV, rfl⟩⟩
802	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff]
803	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map (Over.homMk (homOfLE hWle)))
804	    have hdomle :
805	        (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj (Over.mk ((homOfLE hWle) ≫ Y.hom)) ≤ V := by
806	      intro z hz
807	      obtain ⟨z', hz'V, hz'eq⟩ := (hz : (z : X) ∈ (W : Set X))
808	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'V
809	    convert S.downward_closed hSh (homOfLE hdomle) using 1
810	
811	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is cocontinuous (cover-lifting) from the
812	Grothendieck topology of the open subspace `↥U` to the `U`-slice of the ambient Grothendieck
813	topology. Foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge `overRestrictIso`;
814	fills the `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean` TODO. Project-local. -/
815	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isCocontinuous :
816	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous
817	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) where
818	  cover_lift := by
819	    intro W S hS
820	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
821	    intro y hy
822	    have hpy : (y : X) ∈ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := ⟨y, hy, rfl⟩
823	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf0, hpP⟩ := hS (y : X) hpy
824	    rw [Sieve.overEquiv_iff] at hSf0
825	    have hPle : (P : Set X) ⊆ ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj W).left := leOfHom f
826	    set V : Opens ↥U :=
827	      ⟨Subtype.val ⁻¹' (P : Set X), P.isOpen.preimage continuous_subtype_val⟩ with hVdef
828	    have hVle : V ≤ W := by
829	      intro z hz
830	      obtain ⟨z', hz'W, hz'eq⟩ := hPle (hz : (z : X) ∈ (P : Set X))
831	      exact (Subtype.val_injective hz'eq) ▸ hz'W
832	    refine ⟨V, homOfLE hVle, ?_, hpP⟩
833	    change S ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map (homOfLE hVle))
834	    have hdomle : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj V).left ≤ P := by
835	      intro p hp; obtain ⟨p', hp'V, rfl⟩ := hp; exact hp'V
836	    convert S.downward_closed hSf0 (Over.homMk (homOfLE hdomle) ?_) using 1
837	    · apply Subsingleton.elim
838	
839	/-- The dense-subsite witness for the inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U`, assembled from the two
840	cover-lifting facts above. Project-local glue for `overEquivalence_sheafCongr`. -/
841	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isDenseSubsite :
842	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsDenseSubsite
843	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
844	  Equivalence.isDenseSubsite_inverse_of_isCocontinuous _ _ _
845	
846	/-- The functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
847	inverse and the equivalence adjunction `inverse ⊣ functor`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
848	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
849	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous :
850	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
851	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) := by
852	  haveI : (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.functor.IsCocontinuous
853	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
854	    inferInstanceAs ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsCocontinuous _ _)
855	  exact (Opens.overEquivalence U).symm.toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
856	
857	/-- The inverse of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. Derived from the cocontinuity of the
858	functor and the equivalence adjunction `functor ⊣ inverse`. Needed downstream of `overRestrictIso`
859	for `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`. Project-local. -/
860	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous :
861	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
862	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
863	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).toAdjunction.isContinuous_of_isCocontinuous _ _
864	
865	/-- **The over-site ↔ open-subspace sheaf equivalence.** For a topological space `X`, an open
866	`U ⊆ X`, and any category `A`, the equivalence of sites
867	`Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U`
868	lifts to an equivalence of sheaf categories
869	`Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A`.
870	
871	This is exactly the equivalence left as a TODO in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Over.lean`. It is the
872	foundational layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge (`lem:over_restrict_iso`,
873	`overRestrictIso`): once the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` is identified with the structure
874	sheaf of the open subscheme `U.toScheme` under this equivalence, a presentation of `M.over U`
875	transports (via `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` + `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`) to a geometric
876	presentation of `M|_U`. Project-local: Mathlib supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
877	noncomputable def overEquivalence_sheafCongr (A : Type*) [Category A] :
878	    Sheaf ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) A ≌ Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A :=
879	  (Opens.overEquivalence U).sheafCongr
880	    ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) A
881	
882	end OverSiteSheafEquivalence
883	
884	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the slice-to-geometric module bridge (gap1, C)
885	
886	This section builds the geometric layer of the gap1 slice-to-geometric bridge
887	`lem:over_restrict_iso` on top of the topological `overEquivalence_sheafCongr` of the previous
888	section. It identifies, on the level of *sheaves of modules*, the abstract Grothendieck-slice
889	restriction `M.over U` (a sheaf of modules over the sliced structure sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice
890	site `J.over U`) with the geometric restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M` on the small Zariski
891	site of the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
892	
893	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
894	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
895	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
896	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
897	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
898	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
899	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
900	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
901	
902	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
903	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
904	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
905	
906	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
907	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
908	
909	section OverRestrictBridge
910	
911	open TopologicalSpace Topology
912	
913	namespace Scheme.Modules
914	
915	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
916	
917	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
918	
919	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
920	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
921	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
922	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
923	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
924	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
925	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
926	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
927	
928	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
929	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
930	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
931	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
932	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
933	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
934	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
935	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
936	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
937	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
938	    (by ext : 2
939	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
940	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
941	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
942	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
943	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
944	    (by ext : 2
945	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
946	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
947	          Functor.comp_obj]
948	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
949	        rename_i x
950	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
951	            = 𝟙 _ := by
952	          rw [← op_comp]
953	          simp
954	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
955	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
956	
957	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
958	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
959	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
960	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
961	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
962	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/

771	chart, after which the two affine dictionaries apply. See blueprint
772	`lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read`. -/
773	noncomputable def base_change_mate_codomain_read {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
774	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
775	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
776	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
777	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj
778	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
779	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
780	            (tilde M))) ≅
781	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
782	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
783	            (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
784	        ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
785	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
786	            (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) := by
787	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
788	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
789	  set e := pullbackSpecIso (R := (R : Type u)) (S := (A : Type u)) (T := (R' : Type u)) with he
790	  set inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
791	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))) with hinclA
792	  set inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight
793	    (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom with hinclR'
794	  -- Use `.1`/`.2` projections (not `obtain`) so the body has no stuck `And.casesOn`; this makes
795	  -- `base_change_mate_codomain_read` definitionally the variable-legs read
796	  -- `base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs … pullback.fst pullback.snd …` (proof irrelevance on the
797	  -- leg-equality arguments), which is what lets `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex` reduce to the
798	  -- abstract `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs` by `exact`.
799	  have hfst : Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) = e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclA :=
800	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq e).mp (pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor ψ φ).1
801	  have hsnd : Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) = e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclR' :=
802	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq e).mp (pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor ψ φ).2
803	  set g' := Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) with hg'
804	  set f' := Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ) with hf'
805	  set W₀ := (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map inclA)).obj (tilde M) with hW₀
806	  -- Read the pullback leg `g'` through `pullbackSpecIso` as the affine `Spec inclA` pullback.
807	  have iso_g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj (tilde M)
808	        ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀ :=
809	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hfst).app (tilde M) ≪≫
810	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.hom (Spec.map inclA)).symm.app (tilde M)
811	  -- `e.hom` is an iso of schemes, so pulling back then pushing forward returns the module.
812	  have unit_iso : W₀ ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward e.hom).obj
813	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) :=
814	    (asIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom).unit).app W₀
815	  -- Assemble the object isomorphism `C₀ ≅ tilde ((A⊗R')⊗_A M)`.
816	  refine (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).mapIso ?_ ≪≫
817	    (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclR'.hom).obj
818	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).symm
819	  exact (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').mapIso iso_g ≪≫
820	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr hsnd).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) ≪≫
821	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp e.hom (Spec.map inclR')).symm.app
822	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom).obj W₀) ≪≫
823	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclR')).mapIso unit_iso.symm ≪≫
824	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclR')).mapIso (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M) ≪≫
825	    pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclR' ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)
826	
827	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate (object form).** The bundled `R'`-linear
828	isomorphism of `ModuleCat R'`
829	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\cong\; R' \otimes_R M \]
830	in the exact `extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` packaging used by the codomain/domain reads: the
831	source is `restrictScalars includeRight` of `extendScalars includeLeftRingHom` of `M` (i.e.
832	`(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` read as an `R'`-module through the right tensor factor), and the target is
833	`extendScalars ψ` of `restrictScalars φ` of `M` (i.e. `R' ⊗_R M`). Its inverse is the generator the
834	section-level mate produces (`r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); it carries no flatness hypothesis. See
835	blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`.
836	
837	The mathematical content is the proved, axiom-clean `comm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` core
838	(`TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`, `R'`-linear via the `rightAlgebra` action,
839	no flatness). Here it is transported to the `ModuleCat` change-of-rings objects through the identity
840	`A`-linear bridge `eT` (resolving the `Module A (A ⊗[R] R')` diamond: `extendScalars` uses
841	`restrictScalars includeLeftRingHom` whereas `cancelBaseChange` forces the canonical
842	`Algebra A (A ⊗[R] R')`), so the underlying additive equivalence `g` lands on the genuine
843	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` object carriers; `LinearEquiv.toModuleIso` packages it directly.
844	
845	STATUS (iter-011, route (a) executed): the def is **fully proved, no `sorry`**. The residual
846	`map_smul'` of `g` is discharged by `TensorProduct.induction_on`: the per-generator (`tmul`) identity
847	and `R'`-additivity (`add`) close via `erw [ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.smul_tmul]` (defeq-matching past
848	the opaque object `Module R'`) + a `change` peeling `g` to `cancelBaseChange_tmul`/`comm_tmul`; the
849	two `zero` branches — `g (r' • 0) = r' • g 0`, formerly the carrier-instance wall — close in term
850	mode via `congrArg`/`AddEquiv.map_zero`/`smul_zero` (sidestepping `rw`'s keyed matching) and, for the
851	inner `0 ⊗ₜ m`, `erw [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]` (defeq-matching the diamond instance). Eliminating the
852	`map_smul'` field entirely via the natively `R'`-linear `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` is
853	blocked by the same `A`-action diamond at the `exact`/ascription boundary (the object carrier and the
854	pushout carrier are defeq but not syntactically equal, so neither `exact` nor a `≃ₗ` type ascription
855	unifies them); the present route reaches the same axiom-clean conclusion. -/
856	noncomputable def base_change_mate_regroupEquiv {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
857	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
858	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
859	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
860	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars
861	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
862	          (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
863	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
864	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
865	          (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M) ≅
866	      (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by

872	    ⟨fun r a m => by rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]; rfl⟩
873	  -- `eT`: the identity `A`-linear bridge from the `restrictScalars includeLeftRingHom` `A`-structure
874	  -- on `A ⊗[R] R'` to the canonical `Algebra A (A ⊗[R] R')` structure (resolving the diamond).
875	  let eT : (↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
876	        (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj
877	          (ModuleCat.of ((A : Type u) ⊗[(R : Type u)] (R' : Type u))
878	            ((A : Type u) ⊗[(R : Type u)] (R' : Type u)))))
879	        ≃ₗ[(A : Type u)] ((A : Type u) ⊗[(R : Type u)] (R' : Type u)) :=
880	    { toFun := id, invFun := id, left_inv := fun _ => rfl, right_inv := fun _ => rfl,
881	      map_add' := fun _ _ => rfl, map_smul' := fun a x => by rw [Algebra.smul_def]; rfl }
882	  letI : Algebra (R' : Type u) ((A : Type u) ⊗[(R : Type u)] (R' : Type u)) :=
883	    Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra

885	  letI instLHS : Module (R' : Type u) (↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars
886	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
887	        (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M)) :=
888	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R' : Type u) ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
889	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
890	          (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom).obj
891	      ((ModuleCat.extendScalars
892	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
893	          (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M)))
894	  letI instRHS : Module (R' : Type u) (↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars ψ.hom).obj
895	      (ModuleCat.of (R' : Type u) (R' : Type u))) ⊗[(R : Type u)]
896	        ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)) :=
897	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R' : Type u)
898	      ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)))

900	  -- natively `R'`-linear pushout cancellation `cancelBaseChange` (Mathlib, no flatness). The object
901	  -- carrier produced by the dictionaries tensors `A ⊗[R] R'` over the `restrictScalars
902	  -- includeLeftRingHom` `A`-action, whereas `cancelBaseChange` uses the canonical `Algebra A
903	  -- (A ⊗[R] R')`; the identity `A`-linear bridge `eT` reconciles this lone diamond at the carrier
904	  -- level. The underlying additive equivalence is `comm ≫ congr(refl, eT) ≫ cancelBaseChange ≫ comm`,
905	  -- landing on the genuine object carriers.
906	  let g :
907	      (↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars
908	        (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
909	          (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M))
910	      ≃+ ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)) :=
911	    (TensorProduct.comm (A : Type u) _ ↑M).toAddEquiv.trans
912	      ((TensorProduct.congr (LinearEquiv.refl (A : Type u) ↑M) eT).toAddEquiv.trans
913	        (((TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
914	            (R : Type u) (A : Type u) (A : Type u) ↑M (R' : Type u)).toAddEquiv).trans
915	          (TensorProduct.comm (R : Type u) ↑M (R' : Type u)).toAddEquiv))
916	  refine LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (e := { g with map_smul' := ?_ })
917	  intro r' x
918	  simp only [AddEquiv.toFun_eq_coe, RingHom.id_apply]
919	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
920	  | zero =>
921	      -- `g (r' • 0) = r' • g 0`: both sides are `0`. Term-mode (sidesteps `rw`'s keyed matching,
922	      -- which is blocked by the opaque object `Module ↑R'` instances on the tensor carriers).

924	        (g.map_zero.trans ((smul_zero r').symm.trans (congrArg (r' • ·) g.map_zero.symm)))
925	  | add a b ha hb =>
926	      erw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add]
927	      exact congrArg₂ (· + ·) ha hb
928	  | tmul t m =>
929	    induction t using TensorProduct.induction_on with
930	    | zero =>

933	      -- from the context (so `TensorProduct.zero_tmul` matches), avoiding the `A`-action diamond.
934	      have h0 : g (r' • (0 : ↑((ModuleCat.extendScalars
935	            (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u))
936	              (B := (R' : Type u)))).obj M))) = r' • g 0 :=
937	        (congrArg (⇑g) (smul_zero r')).trans
938	          (g.map_zero.trans ((smul_zero r').symm.trans (congrArg (r' • ·) g.map_zero.symm)))
939	      erw [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
940	      exact (congrArg (⇑g) (smul_zero r')).trans
941	        (g.map_zero.trans ((smul_zero r').symm.trans (congrArg (r' • ·) g.map_zero.symm)))
942	    | add a b ha hb =>
943	      erw [TensorProduct.add_tmul, smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add]

945	    | tmul a s =>
946	      -- The `R'`-action on the source is `restrictScalars includeRight` of the `A ⊗[R] R'`-module,
947	      -- so `r' • z = (1 ⊗ r') • z` definitionally; `erw` reduces the `A ⊗[R] R'`-module smul on the
948	      -- left factor up to that defeq: `(1 ⊗ r') • (a ⊗ s) = (1 ⊗ r') * (a ⊗ s) = a ⊗ (r' * s)`.

950	        show (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight r' : (↑A ⊗[↑R] ↑R')) = (1 : ↑A) ⊗ₜ[↑R] r' from rfl,
951	        Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul, one_mul]

957	          ((TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange ↑R ↑A ↑A ↑M ↑R')
958	            (m ⊗ₜ[↑A] (a ⊗ₜ[↑R] s)))

960	        TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_tmul,
961	        TensorProduct.comm_tmul, TensorProduct.comm_tmul]

965	/-! ## Section-level mate identity, decomposed into three seams
966	
967	The LHS unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` on global sections splits at three categorical
968	seams (blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_unit_value` / `…_fstar_reindex` / `…_gstar_transpose`):
969	
970	* Seam 1 — the affine pullback–pushforward unit IS the algebraic unit `η_M`;
971	* Seam 2 — the pushforward pseudofunctor reindex of the inner comparison;
972	* Seam 3 — the `(g^* ⊣ g_*)` transpose of the comparison on sections.
973	
974	`base_change_mate_section_identity` is then the one-line chain of the counit factorization
975	(`Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`) and Seam 3. -/
976	
977	-- The conjugate-unit calculus chains several `erw` defeq-unifications and a `simp` closure over the
978	-- `restrictScalars`/tilde–Γ round trips, which collectively exceed the default heartbeat budget.
979	set_option maxHeartbeats 4000000 in
980	/-- **Seam 1: the affine pullback–pushforward unit is the algebraic unit.** Let
981	`ι_A : A → A ⊗_R R'` be the canonical inclusion. The unit of the
982	`((Spec ι_A)^*, (Spec ι_A)_*)`-adjunction evaluated at `tilde M`, read on global sections over
983	`Spec A` through the two tilde dictionaries (`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`,
984	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`) and the tilde–Γ unit, equals the algebraic unit
985	`η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m` (Mathlib's `ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj`
986	unit). See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_unit_value`. -/
987	theorem base_change_mate_unit_value {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
988	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
989	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
990	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
991	    let inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
992	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))
993	    (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).hom ≫
994	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
995	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)).unit.app (tilde M)) ≫
996	        ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).mapIso
997	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).mapIso
998	                (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M) ≪≫
999	              pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))
1000	          ≪≫ (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app
1001	                ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).obj
1002	                  ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).symm).hom
1003	        = (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M := by
1004	  -- REMAINING (the affine, square-free heart): a conjugate-adjunction unit coherence. The
1005	  -- geometric adjunction `((Spec ι_A)^* ⊣ (Spec ι_A)_*)` transports, under the two tilde
1006	  -- dictionaries — `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (identifying `(Spec ι_A)^*` of a tilde with
1007	  -- `extendScalars ι_A`) and `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (identifying `(Spec ι_A)_*` of a tilde
1008	  -- with `restrictScalars ι_A`) — to the algebraic adjunction
1009	  -- `(extendScalars ι_A ⊣ restrictScalars ι_A) = ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj ι_A`. Since
1010	  -- `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` is by construction `((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm
1011	  -- (gammaPushforwardNatIso ι_A)).symm.app`, the LHS is the image of the geometric unit under the
1012	  -- comparison, and `Adjunction.conjugateEquiv` / `homEquiv` naturality identify it with the
1013	  -- algebraic unit evaluated at `M` (`m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`). The element-level actions of the two
1014	  -- dictionaries are opaque (built via `conjugateIsoEquiv`), so the closure needs the abstract
1015	  -- conjugate-unit coherence, not an `ext`-chase. Uses: `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`,
1016	  -- `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`, `Adjunction.conjugateEquiv`.
1017	  intro inclA
1018	  -- The two composed adjunctions, exactly as in `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` (with `φ := inclA`).
1019	  set adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := A)).comp
1020	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)) with hadjL
1021	  set adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).comp
1022	    (tilde.adjunction (R := _)) with hadjR
1023	  -- Move 1: the first two factors are the unit of `adjL`.
1024	  have hunitL : adjL.unit.app M
1025	      = (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).hom ≫
1026	          (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
1027	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map inclA)).unit.app (tilde M)) := by
1028	    rw [hadjL, Adjunction.comp_unit_app]
1029	    rfl
1030	  -- Move 3: the unit of `adjR` splits off the algebraic unit `η_M`.
1031	  have hunitR : adjR.unit.app M
1032	      = (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M ≫
1033	          (ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).map
1034	            ((tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom) := by
1035	    rw [hadjR, Adjunction.comp_unit_app]
1036	    rfl
1037	  -- The right-adjoint comparison nat-iso `β : R₁ ≅ R₂` fed to the conjugate calculus.
1038	  set β := gammaPushforwardNatIso inclA with hβ
1039	  -- Move 2: the conjugate-unit coherence. `((conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm β.hom).app M` is, by the
1040	  -- definition of `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via `conjugateIsoEquiv`, exactly `pullback_spec_tilde_iso⁻¹`.
1041	  have hpullinv : ((conjugateEquiv adjL adjR).symm β.hom).app M
1042	      = (pullback_spec_tilde_iso inclA M).inv := by rw [hβ]; rfl
1043	  have huce := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv_symm adjL adjR β.hom M
1044	  rw [hpullinv] at huce
1045	  -- Move 4, part 1 (Claim A): the pushforward dictionary `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`, read on Γ and
1046	  -- composed with the tilde–Γ unit, is the Γ-fragment comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso`. This is
1047	  -- the right-triangle identity of the tilde ⊣ Γ adjunction (`fromTildeΓ` is its counit).
1048	  have htri : (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1049	        ((pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).obj (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))))
1050	      = (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).obj
1051	          ((pushforward (Spec.map inclA)).obj
1052	            (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))))).inv :=
1053	    (Iso.hom_comp_eq_id _).mp (tilde.adjunction.right_triangle_components _)
1054	  have hClaimA : (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := A)).map
1055	        (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom ≫
1056	        (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).obj
1057	          ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).inv
1058	      = (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom := by
1059	    rw [Iso.comp_inv_eq, pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule]
1060	    simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_inv, Functor.mapIso_hom, Functor.map_comp,
1061	      Functor.map_inv, IsIso.inv_comp_eq]
1062	    rw [htri]
1063	    exact (NatIso.naturality_1 tilde.toTildeΓNatIso
1064	      (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom).symm
1065	  -- Move 4, part 2: `β.hom` at a tilde object is the per-object `gammaPushforwardIso`, and
1066	  -- `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` factors through it and the tilde–Γ unit.
1067	  have hβapp : β.hom.app (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))
1068	      = (gammaPushforwardIso inclA (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M))).hom := by
1069	    rw [hβ, gammaPushforwardNatIso]; simp
1070	  have hgPTI : (gammaPushforwardTildeIso inclA ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom
1071	      = β.hom.app (tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)) ≫
1072	        (ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclA.hom).map
1073	          (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).inv := by
1074	    rw [hβapp, gammaPushforwardTildeIso]
1075	    simp [Iso.trans_hom]
1076	  -- Final assembly. Move 1 folds the first two factors into `adjL.unit`; unfolding the bracket and
1077	  -- applying Claim A + hgPTI rewrites it through `β`; β-naturality at `pullback_spec_tilde_iso.hom`
1078	  -- and the conjugate-unit identity `huce` then collapse everything to the algebraic unit `η_M`.
1079	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← hunitL]
1080	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]
1081	  rw [hClaimA]
1082	  -- Finish: hgPTI expands the Γ-fragment comparison; converting to composed-functor form
1083	  -- (`← Functor.comp_map`) lets the β-naturality square (`erw [β.hom.naturality_assoc]`) and the
1084	  -- conjugate-unit identity (`erw [reassoc_of% huce]`) push the geometric unit through `β`; the
1085	  -- tilde–Γ and pullback-iso round trips then cancel, leaving the algebraic unit `η_M` (hunitR).
1086	  rw [hgPTI]
1087	  simp only [← Functor.comp_map]
1088	  erw [β.hom.naturality_assoc]
1089	  erw [reassoc_of% huce]
1090	  rw [hunitR]
1091	  simp only [Functor.comp_map]
1092	  simp [← Functor.map_comp]
1093	  rw [← Iso.app_hom, ← Iso.app_inv, Iso.hom_inv_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, Category.comp_id]
1094	
1095	/-- **The `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of the inner pushforward comparison.** The canonical
1096	`R`-linear map `M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`, viewed as a morphism of `ModuleCat R`
1097	into the `restrictScalars ψ`/`restrictScalars inclR'` packaging of the codomain read. It is built
1098	as `restrictScalars φ` of the algebraic unit `η_M = extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA` (Seam 1's
1099	value), transported across the ring equation `inclA ∘ φ = inclR' ∘ ψ` (both equal
1100	`algebraMap R (A ⊗_R R')`) by the change-of-rings tower isos. This is the `ρ` appearing on the RHS
1101	of Seam 2 (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`). -/
1102	noncomputable def base_change_mate_inner_value {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1103	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1104	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
1105	    letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
1106	    let inclA := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom
1107	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u)))
1108	    let inclR' := CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight
1109	      (R := (R : Type u)) (A := (A : Type u)) (B := (R' : Type u))).toRingHom
1110	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M ⟶
1111	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars ψ.hom).obj
1112	        ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars inclR'.hom).obj
1113	          ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)) := by
1114	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
1115	  letI : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
1116	  intro inclA inclR'
1117	  have hring : inclA.hom.comp φ.hom = inclR'.hom.comp ψ.hom := by
1118	    ext r
1119	    show (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) r ⊗ₜ[(R : Type u)] (1 : (R' : Type u))
1120	        = (1 : (A : Type u)) ⊗ₜ[(R : Type u)] (CommRingCat.Hom.hom ψ) r
1121	    have ha : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom φ) r = r • (1 : (A : Type u)) :=
1122	      Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one r
1123	    have hb : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom ψ) r = r • (1 : (R' : Type u)) :=
1124	      Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one r
1125	    rw [ha, hb, TensorProduct.smul_tmul]
1126	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).map
1127	        ((ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj inclA.hom).unit.app M) ≫
1128	      ((ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom inclA.hom (inclA.hom.comp φ.hom) rfl
1129	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).symm ≪≫
1130	        (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hring).app
1131	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M) ≪≫
1132	        ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App ψ.hom inclR'.hom (inclR'.hom.comp ψ.hom) rfl
1133	            ((ModuleCat.extendScalars inclA.hom).obj M)).hom
1134	
1135	/-- **Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback–pushforward unit.** For composable scheme morphisms
1136	`a : X₁ ⟶ X₂`, `b : X₂ ⟶ X₃` and a module `N` on `X₁`, the unit of the
1137	`(pullback (a ≫ b) ⊣ pushforward (a ≫ b))`-adjunction factors through the units of `a` and `b`
1138	together with the `pushforwardComp`/`pullbackComp` coherences. This is the abstract mate identity
1139	`unit_conjugateEquiv` specialised by `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`
1140	(`conjugateEquiv … (pullbackComp a b).inv = (pushforwardComp a b).hom`). It is the leg-reindex
1141	engine of Seam 2: with `a := e.hom` (an iso) and `b := Spec ι_A`, it turns the generic
1142	`(pullback.fst)`-unit into the affine `(Spec ι_A)`-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1143	Project-local. -/
1144	theorem pullbackPushforward_unit_comp {X₁ X₂ X₃ : Scheme.{u}} (a : X₁ ⟶ X₂) (b : X₂ ⟶ X₃)
1145	    (N : X₃.Modules) :
1146	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1147	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1148	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
1149	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj N)) ≫
1150	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp a b).hom.app _
1151	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N ≫
1152	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (a ≫ b)).map
1153	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp a b).inv.app N) := by
1154	  have h := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv

1319	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (a ≫ b)).unit.app N
1320	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app N ≫
1321	          (Scheme.Modules.pushforward b).map
1322	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
