1964	regrouping); since `cancelBaseChange` is a Mathlib `LinearEquiv` with no flatness hypothesis, the
1965	conjugate is an isomorphism. This `IsIso` form is the iso-consequence the affine close consumes
1966	(mirroring the parent `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`); the proof obligation is the
1967	adjoint-mate unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` on global sections through the two proved
1968	tilde dictionaries. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace`. -/
1969	theorem base_change_mate_generator_trace {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
1970	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1971	    IsIso ((base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1972	      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1973	        (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1974	          (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1975	          (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1976	          (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1977	      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom) := by
1978	  -- By the section identity `base_change_mate_section_identity` the conjugated map equals
1979	  -- `(base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`, the inverse of a `ModuleCat` isomorphism, hence an
1980	  -- isomorphism.
1981	  rw [base_change_mate_section_identity]
1982	  infer_instance
1983	
1984	/-! ## Section-level value of the affine base-change map -/
1985	
1986	/-- **Section-level value of the affine base-change map.** In the affine–affine model — base ring
1987	maps `ψ : R ⟶ R'` (the base change `g = Spec ψ`) and `φ : R ⟶ A` (the affine morphism
1988	`f = Spec φ`), with `F = M^~` for an `A`-module `M`, the canonical pullback square supplying
1989	`g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd` — the global-sections incarnation `Γ(α)` of the
1990	base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism of `R'`-modules.
1991	
1992	By the four affine dictionaries the domain `Γ(g^*(f_* M^~))` reads as `R' ⊗_R M` and the codomain
1993	`Γ(f'_*(g')^* M^~)` reads as `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M`, under which `Γ(α)` is the canonical cancellation
1994	isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
1995	`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no
1996	flatness hypothesis, `Γ(α)` is an isomorphism. This is the section-level content of the affine
1997	close: it is the per-affine-chart input to `base_change_map_affine_local` once the locality
1998	reduction has restricted an arbitrary square to this affine–affine model. See blueprint
1999	`lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`.
2000	
2001	The proof is the 4-step generator trace of the blueprint: unwind the adjoint mate of the
2002	`((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit on global sections through `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and
2003	`pullback_spec_tilde_iso`, landing on `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`. This theorem's **body has no inline
2004	`sorry`** (it conjugates `base_change_mate_generator_trace` back through the domain/codomain reads),
2005	but it is **transitively `sorry`-backed** through `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. The genuine
2006	crux — the mate-unwinding coherence over the generic pullback square
2007	`pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` — is the outstanding obligation carried by
2008	`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (and, beneath it, `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs`), NOT
2009	here. -/
2010	theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange
2011	    {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
2012	    IsIso ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
2013	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2014	        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2015	        (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2016	        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M))) := by
2017	  -- Assemble the chain (blueprint `lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`): the domain
2018	  -- read `Θ_src` (`base_change_mate_domain_read`) and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
2019	  -- (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, resting on the `pullbackSpecIso` leg identification
2020	  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`) identify `Γ(α)`'s domain/codomain with `R' ⊗_R M` and
2021	  -- `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`; the generator trace `base_change_mate_generator_trace` shows the conjugate
2022	  -- `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt` is an isomorphism (it is `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, no flatness).
2023	  -- Conjugating back, `Γ(α)` is itself an isomorphism.
2024	  haveI hconj := base_change_mate_generator_trace ψ φ M
2025	  set D := base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M with hD
2026	  set C := base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M with hC
2027	  set Γα := (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
2028	    (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2029	      (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2030	      (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2031	      (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) with hΓα
2032	  have heq : Γα = D.hom ≫ (D.inv ≫ Γα ≫ C.hom) ≫ C.inv := by
2033	    simp [Category.assoc]
2034	  rw [heq]
2035	  infer_instance
2036	
2037	/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/
2038	
2039	/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
2040	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
2041	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
2042	the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
2043	affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
2044	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
2045	by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
2046	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
2047	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
2048	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
2049	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2050	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2051	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
2052	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
2053	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H
2054	
2055	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
2056	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
2057	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
2058	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
2059	
2060	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
2061	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2062	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2063	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
2064	  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
2065	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
2066	  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine–affine
2067	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
2068	  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
2069	  intro U
2070	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.
2071	  --
2072	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
2073	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
2074	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
2075	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
2076	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
2077	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
2078	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
2079	  --
2080	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
2081	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
2082	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
2083	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
2084	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
2085	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
2086	  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
2087	  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the
2088	  -- tilde–Γ counit isos to pass `IsIso Γ(α) ⟹ IsIso α` on the QC chart). This restriction-
2089	  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining
2090	  -- multi-hundred-LOC build for the unconditional general theorem.
2091	  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
2092	  sorry
2093	
2094	/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
2095	and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
2096	isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2097	with `A → B` flat, the comparison map `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B → H⁰(X_B, F_B)` is an
2098	isomorphism.
2099	
2100	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH ("Flat base change"), the `i = 0` case. -/
2101	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
2102	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2103	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
2104	  -- Proof strategy (Stacks 02KH, `i = 0`), deferred to a later iteration:
2105	  -- the statement is local on `S'`, so reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2106	  -- with `A → B` flat.  Choose a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`.  Since `f`
2107	  -- is quasi-compact and quasi-separated the Čech complex of `𝒰` computes
2108	  -- `H⁰(X, F)`, and base change identifies `Čech(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Čech(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`
2109	  -- term by term via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.  Flatness of `A → B`
2110	  -- makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `H⁰`, giving the isomorphism
2111	  -- `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)`.  Needs the (missing) Čech-cohomology /
2112	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see
2113	  -- `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
2114	  sorry
2115	
2116	end AlgebraicGeometry

819	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data
820	
821	We assemble the affine charts, their principal-open overlaps, the transition
822	isomorphisms and the cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by
823	the size-`d` subsets `{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`. Its `.glued` is the
824	Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
825	
826	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
827	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
828	
829	/-- The triple-overlap `t'`-field of the Grassmannian glue data
830	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the morphism
831	`U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K ⟶ U^J_K ×_{U^J} U^J_I` reconciling the two pullbacks via the
832	away-pullback identification, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`, and the
833	order-swap isomorphism. Project-local. -/
834	noncomputable def chartTransition' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
835	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
836	    Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ⟶
837	      Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) :=
838	  (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
839	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
840	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
841	      (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
842	    (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).inv
843	
844	/-- `awayMulCommEquiv` lies over the base ring: it intertwines the two structure maps
845	`R → R[1/(xy)]` and `R → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for the `t_fac`/cocycle
846	ring computations. -/
847	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
848	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (x * y)))
849	      = algebraMap A (Localization.Away (y * x)) := by
850	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (y * x) (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
851	    rw [mul_comm y x]; infer_instance
852	  ext a
853	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (y * x))
854	    (Localization.Away (x * y)) (Localization.Away (y * x))).commutes a
855	
856	/-- The ring-hom identity underlying the `t_fac` coherence field of the Grassmannian
857	glue data: over the triple-overlap rings, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`
858	pre-composed with the order-swap and right inclusion equals the left inclusion
859	post-composed with the plain transition `θ_{I,J}`. Both reduce to
860	`ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
861	theorem chartTransition'_ringIdentity (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
862	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
863	    (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
864	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
865	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
866	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
867	          (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
868	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r J I hJ hI))
869	  -- Reduce both sides to `ι^L ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` after precomposing with the structure map of
870	  -- `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
871	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap,
872	    awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
873	    cocycleΘIJ, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
874	    transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
875	
876	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
877	-- The `erw` through the `HasPullback` instance diamond on the heavy `MvPolynomial`
878	-- localisation objects is defeq-expensive; the raised limit covers it.
879	/-- The `t_fac`-compatibility field of the Grassmannian glue data
880	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the triple-overlap transition `t'` is compatible with the
881	projections, `t'_{I,J,K} ≫ pr₂ = pr₁ ≫ t_{I,J}`. Reduces, both pullbacks being
882	affine, to the ring identity `chartTransition'_ringIdentity` via the leg lemmas.
883	Project-local. -/
884	theorem chartTransition'_fac (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
885	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
886	    chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
887	        Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
888	      = Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ≫
889	          chartTransition d r I J hI hJ := by
890	  -- Rewrite the source `pr₁` via the iso (keeping a single `awayPullbackIso` term so the
891	  -- `HasPullback` instance is consistent), then cancel the leg via the iso identity.
892	  have hfstc : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
893	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
894	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
895	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
896	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
897	  have hfst := (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hfstc
898	  -- The pure ring/`Spec` content: the three localised pieces equal the plain transition.
899	  have hXY : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
900	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
901	          (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
902	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
903	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)))
904	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
905	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) ≫
906	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionMap d r I J hI hJ)) := by
907	    rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
908	      ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, chartTransition'_ringIdentity]
909	  rw [hfst, chartTransition']
910	  simp only [Category.assoc]
911	  -- `erw` (defeq) to fire the snd-leg lemma through the `HasPullback` instance diamond.
912	  erw [awayPullbackIso_inv_snd]
913	  simp only [chartTransition]
914	  -- `congrArg` + defeq associativity closes it (syntactic `rw`/`Category.assoc` are blocked
915	  -- by the Scheme-category instance diamond on these heavy localisation objects).
916	  exact congrArg (_ ≫ ·) hXY
917	
918	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the rotated triple-overlap ring cocycle `Φ = id`
919	
920	The `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data reduces (after stripping the conjugating
921	pullback isomorphisms) to a single ring identity `Φ = id` over the triple-overlap ring
922	`S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`, where
923	`Φ := Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I` (rotated index triples).
924	We prove it by telescoping with the cocycle condition `cocycleCondition` (collapsing
925	`θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K} = θ_{I,K}`) down to a single inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`,
926	which is closed by the matrix computation `transitionInvImageMatrix` (the `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` collapse
927	of `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq`, run for the inverse pair).
928	
929	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id`
930	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
931	
932	/-- The order-swap absorbs a left away-inclusion into a right one:
933	`swap_{x,y} ∘ ι^L_{x,y} = ι^R_{y,x}` as maps `R[1/x] → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for
934	the rotation lemma `rotMid`. -/
935	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
936	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (awayInclLeft x y) = awayInclRight y x := by
937	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers x)
938	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap,
939	    awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap]
940	
941	/-- **Rotation lemma** for the triple-overlap transitions: conjugating the rotated
942	`Θ_{J,K,I}` by the two order-swaps recovers the `J,K`-transition `Θ_{J,K}` in the `I,J,K`
943	frame. Both sides are lifts of `θ̃_{J,K}`; checked on the chart ring `R^K`. Project-local. -/
944	private lemma rotMid (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
945	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
946	    ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp
947	        (cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI)).comp
948	        (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom
949	      = cocycleΘJK d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
950	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext
951	    (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ))
952	  rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, RingHom.comp_assoc, cocycleΘIJ,
953	    IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft,
954	    cocycleΘJK, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
955	
956	/-- The matrix collapse behind the inverse pair `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id`: pushing the image
957	matrix `(X^K_I)⁻¹ X^K` of `θ_{K,I}` forward along `θ_{I,K}` (realised as
958	`cocycleΘIK ∘ ι^L`) recovers the universal matrix `X^I` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I`.
959	Both reduce to `W = X^I` over `S_I` via the `(W_K)⁻¹ W` computation, using `W_I = 1`.
960	Project-local. -/
961	private lemma transitionInvImageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
962	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
963	    (imageMatrix d r K I hK hI).map
964	        ((cocycleΘIK d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
965	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)))
966	      = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
967	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)

1022	/-- The inverse-pair ring identity `θ_{I,K} ∘ θ_{K,I} = id` over the triple-overlap ring

1061	`Θ_{I,J,K} ∘ swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K ∘ Θ_{K,I,J} ∘ swap_I` is the identity. This is the
1062	ring identity underlying the `cocycle` field of the Grassmannian glue data. Proved by
1063	telescoping: the middle `swap_J ∘ Θ_{J,K,I} ∘ swap_K` collapses to `Θ_{J,K}` (`rotMid`), then

1067	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :

1069	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp

1071	            ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI) (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom.comp
1072	              ((cocycleΘIJ d r K I J hK hI hJ).comp
1073	                (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).toRingHom))))

1076	        ((awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom.comp

1100	-- over the heavy `MvPolynomial` away-localisation objects is defeq-expensive; raised limit.
1101	/-- The **scheme-level cocycle** field of the Grassmannian glue data
1102	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the threefold composite of triple-overlap transitions is the
1103	identity. The two internal conjugating-pullback pairs cancel (`Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc`),
1104	the six `Spec`-arrows collapse into a single `Spec` of the rotated ring cocycle `Φ`, and
1105	`cocyclePhiId` (`Φ = id`) closes it, leaving `ap.hom ≫ ap.inv = 𝟙`. Project-local. -/
1106	theorem chartTransition'_cocycle (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1107	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1108	    chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫ chartTransition' d r J K I hJ hK hI ≫
1109	        chartTransition' d r K I J hK hI hJ
1110	      = CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id
1111	          (Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)) := by
1112	  have h6 : Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
1113	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
1114	          (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
1115	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r J K I hJ hK hI)) ≫
1116	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r K I hK hI)
1117	          (minorDet d r K J hK hJ)).toRingHom) ≫
1118	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r K I J hK hI hJ)) ≫
1119	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1120	          (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).toRingHom)
1121	      = CategoryTheory.CategoryStruct.id (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1122	          (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)))) := by

1124	      ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,

1130	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data and scheme
1131	
1132	Assemble the affine charts, principal-open overlaps, transition isomorphisms, and the
1133	cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1134	`{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`; its `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)`
1135	over `ℤ`. Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`. -/
1136	
1137	/-- The **Grassmannian glue data** (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the `Scheme.GlueData` whose
1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued
1159	
1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
1187	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
1188	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
1189	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
1190	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1191	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1192	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1193	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1194	  rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
1195	
1196	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map** `δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J`
1197	(`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism of the restricted diagonal
1198	`Δ^{-1}(U^I ×_ℤ U^J) = U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J`. It is the tensor-product lift of the
1199	structure map `R^I → R^I_J` (first factor) and the pre-localisation transition hom
1200	`θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (second factor), so `X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I` and
1201	`1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
1202	noncomputable def diagonalRingMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :=
1204	  Algebra.TensorProduct.lift
1205	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1206	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
1207	    (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
1208	    (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)
1209	
1210	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the left factor is the structure map: `δ_{I,J}(a ⊗ 1) = a` in `R^I_J`.
1211	Project-local. -/
1212	theorem diagonalRingMap_left (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1213	    (hJ : J.card = d) (a : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :
1214	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] 1)
1215	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a := by
1216	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, mul_one]
1217	  rfl
1218	
1219	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the right factor is the pre-localisation transition hom:
1220	`δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ b) = θ̃_{I,J}(b)`. Project-local. -/
1221	theorem diagonalRingMap_right (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1222	    (hJ : J.card = d) (b : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ) :
1223	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (1 ⊗ₜ[ℤ] b) = transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ b := by
1224	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, one_mul]
1225	
1226	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map is surjective** (`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism
1227	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J` of the restricted diagonal is surjective, so the
1228	restricted diagonal `U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J` is a closed immersion. The image contains the
1229	structure-map image of `R^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (right
1230	factor), which together generate `R^I_J = R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local. -/
1231	theorem diagonalRingMap_surjective (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1232	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Function.Surjective (diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
1233	  intro z
1234	  -- `z · uⁿ = algebraMap a` for some `a` and `n = ` power of `P^I_J`, by the localisation
1235	  -- property, where `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1236	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) z
1237	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1238	  -- The witness pushes the power into the second tensor factor: `a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ`, avoiding
1239	  -- any arithmetic in the tensor-product ring.
1240	  refine ⟨a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (minorDet d r J I hJ hI ^ n), ?_⟩
1241	  -- `v := θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is the inverse of `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.

1336	    show CommRingCat.ofHom (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
1337	        = CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)

1352	/-- The structure morphism `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **separated** (`lem:gr_separated`, morphism form).
1353	Following the Proj template (`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.isSeparated`): on each patch
1354	`U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight` the restricted diagonal is, via

1358	  refine ⟨IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.of_openCover (Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeftRight
1359	    (theGlueData d r).openCover (theGlueData d r).openCover (toSpecZ d r) (toSpecZ d r)) ?_⟩
1360	  intro ⟨i, j⟩
1361	  dsimp only [Scheme.Cover.pullbackHom]
1362	  refine (MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := @IsClosedImmersion)
1363	    (f := (pullbackDiagonalMapIdIso ..).inv) _).mp ?_
1364	  let e₁ : pullback ((theGlueData d r).openCover.f i ≫ toSpecZ d r)
1365	        ((theGlueData d r).openCover.f j ≫ toSpecZ d r) ≅
1366	        Spec (.of (TensorProduct ℤ
1367	          (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ)
1368	          (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ j.1}) ℤ))) :=
1369	    pullback.congrHom (ι_toSpecZ d r i) (ι_toSpecZ d r j) ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso ℤ _ _
1370	  let e₂ : pullback ((theGlueData d r).openCover.f i) ((theGlueData d r).openCover.f j) ≅
1371	        Spec (.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2))) :=
1372	    pullbackιIso d r i j
1373	  rw [← MorphismProperty.cancel_right_of_respectsIso (P := @IsClosedImmersion) _ e₁.hom,
1374	    ← MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := @IsClosedImmersion) e₂.inv]
1375	  let F : TensorProduct ℤ
1376	        (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ)
1377	        (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ j.1}) ℤ) →+*
1378	        Localization.Away (minorDet d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2) :=
1379	    (diagonalRingMap d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2).toRingHom

1384	  · simp only [e₂, e₁, Iso.trans_hom, pullback.congrHom_hom, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id,

1386	    rw [pullback.lift_fst, Category.comp_id]
1387	    erw [pullbackDiagonalMapIdIso_inv_snd_fst]
1388	    erw [pullbackιIso_inv_fst]

1391	    rw [← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
1392	    congr 1
1393	    refine RingHom.ext fun a => ?_
1394	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom_apply]
1395	    exact (diagonalRingMap_left d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 a).symm
1396	  · simp only [e₂, e₁, Iso.trans_hom, pullback.congrHom_hom, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id,
1397	      Category.comp_id, pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd, ← Spec.map_comp]

1400	    erw [pullbackιIso_inv_snd]

1403	    rw [← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]

1406	    exact (diagonalRingMap_right d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 b).symm

1408	/-- **The Grassmannian `Gr(d,r)` is separated over `ℤ`** (`lem:gr_separated`): the keystone
1409	separatedness statement. Since the Grassmannian scheme is a `Scheme.{0}`, `Spec ℤ` is genuinely
1410	terminal (`specZIsTerminal`); the structure morphism `toSpecZ` is separated
1411	(`isSeparatedToSpecZ`) and `Spec ℤ` is affine (hence separated over the terminal), so the
1412	composite `toSpecZ ≫ (Spec ℤ → ⊤)` — which is the terminal map of `Gr(d,r)` — is separated. -/
1413	theorem isSeparated (d r : ℕ) : (scheme d r).IsSeparated := by
1414	  have hsep : IsSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := isSeparatedToSpecZ d r
1415	  rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1416	  have he : Limits.terminal.from (scheme d r)
1417	      = toSpecZ d r ≫ Limits.terminal.from (Spec (CommRingCat.of ℤ)) :=
1418	    Limits.terminal.hom_ext _ _
1419	  rw [he]
1420	  infer_instance

975	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric

998	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
999	
1000	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1001	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1002	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1003	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1004	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1005	
1006	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1007	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1008	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1009	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/
1020	
1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/
1154	
1155	section BasicOpenPresentationDescent
1156	
1157	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1158	
1159	namespace Scheme.Modules
1160	
1161	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1162	
1163	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1164	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1165	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1166	/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1167	
1168	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
1169	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
1170	`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
1171	then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1172	
1173	It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
1174	`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
1175	over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
1176	needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
1177	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
1178	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1179	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
1180	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
1181	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1182	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
1183	  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
1184	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
1185	      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
1186	      (SheafOfModules.pushforward
1187	        (𝟙 ((show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))
1188	
1189	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
1203	  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _
1208	
1209	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
1210	`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` fact needed by
1211	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Supplied via `haveI` at use sites (instance resolution cannot invert
1212	`φ.inv.base`). Project-local. -/
1213	theorem opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1214	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
1215	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
1216	  infer_instance
1217	
1218	/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).
1219	
1220	For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
1221	structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
1222	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
1223	of schemes is `Final` (`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso`). This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum
1224	consumed by `Presentation.map` along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
1225	Project-local. -/
1226	noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1227	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1228	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by
1229	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := opensMap_final_of_schemeIso φ
1230	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.pushforward (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance
1231	  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
1232	
1233	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1234	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1235	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1236	/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).
1237	
1238	Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
1239	`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
1240	`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
1241	`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:
1242	applied with `φ` the `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` of the affine restriction, it moves the presentation onto
1243	a genuine `Spec`. Project-local. -/
1244	noncomputable def presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z)
1245	    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
1246	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
1247	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1} (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv) :=
1248	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.inv).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
1249	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
1250	    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ)
1251	
1252	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1253	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1254	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1255	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1256	
1257	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
1258	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
1259	`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
1260	the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
1261	a geometric tilde.
1262	
1263	This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
1264	the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
1265	`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
1266	presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
1267	isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
1268	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
1269	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
1270	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
1271	    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
1272	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
1273	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1274	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
1275	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
1276	    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
1277	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
1279	      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))
1280	
1281	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1282	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1283	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1284	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
1285	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).
1286	
1287	Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
1288	`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
1289	`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
1290	`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is
1291	`Spec R_r` since `D(r)` is affine) — has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit, i.e. `M|_{D(r)}` is a
1292	geometric tilde.
1293	
1294	This is the per-element step of gap1: it is the affine instance `W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` of
1295	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, with affineness of `W` from
1296	`IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen` (`D(r)` is affine in `Spec R`) and
1297	`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (its preimage under the open immersion `(q.X i).ι` is
1298	affine, using `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`). Project-local. -/
1299	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1300	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (r : R) (i : q.I)
1301	    (hr : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :
1302	    IsIso (@Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1303	      (Γ(↑(q.X i), (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))
1304	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
1305	          (((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
1306	            (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr)).isoSpec.inv)).obj
1307	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι
1308	            ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))).obj
1309	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)))) :=
1310	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback M q i
1311	    ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r))
1312	    ((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
1313	      (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr))
1314	
1315	end Scheme.Modules
